title: Fold the independent tier-judge into the verifier; the author does not self-tier
context: HDR-010's amendment requires tier judgment to come from an independent agent (fresh context, model != author, 'same independence rule as the verification lane'). The first C design made this a SEPARATE tier-judge subagent dispatched alongside the verifier at the completion gate. An independent leanness review (2026-06-23) flagged this as two near-identical dispatches reading the same committed diff at the same moment.

options.name: separate-tier-judge
options.rationale: A dedicated judge subagent + judge-protocol.md, fully isolated from the verifier.
options.tradeoff: Doubles per-task agent dispatches for any AgDR-emitting task; re-derives the context the verifier already built; a whole new protocol to maintain.

options.name: fold-into-verifier
options.rationale: The verifier already runs at the gate the design needs (success -> awaiting_verification -> verifier pass -> done) and is already model != author (verifier_tier_for forces it). Tier calibration becomes a secondary section of verifier-protocol.md. Because the verifier is the sole authority and always runs for AgDR-emitting tasks, the author need not self-assess a tier at all (untagged = flag-async, fail-safe).
options.tradeoff: One less degree of isolation (a verifier miscalibrated on correctness could be correlated-miscalibrated on tier); slight prompt-attention load, mitigated by verdict-first/calibration-second ordering.

reasoning: HDR-010 requires independence FROM THE AUTHOR, which the verifier already satisfies; it does not require independence from the verifier. Correctness and tier-substance are the same deep read of the same diff, so a separate judge re-derives context. Folding deletes judge-protocol.md, halves dispatches, removes the author self-tier/override/feedback machinery, and leaves task-protocol.md untouched. Since this layer is reviewed-not-tested and exercised live in D regardless, the isolation a second dispatch buys is not worth its cost.

steelman.strongest_case: Full isolation means a verifier whose attention is consumed by the correctness verdict cannot drag down tier calibration; if the two jobs ever prove to interfere in practice, a dedicated judge is the clean separation.

blast_radius: Orchestration-layer only. Adds a section to verifier-protocol.md (reviewed-not-tested); no engine code, no schema change. Reversible: a separate judge can be reintroduced later without undoing this.
